title: composition_rule (v1)
description: A literature-confirmed substrate composition rule. Mirrors and enriches techne/registry/compositions.jsonl (the existing JSONL uses literature_confirmation: [str]; v1 schema uses literature_confirmation: [{citation, context}] for richer provenance). Speculative compositions go in narrative; only confirmed compositions go in this block.

name.title: Composition name (descriptive snake_case)
name.description: snake_case name describing the composition pattern. Examples: 'TierB_x_TierD_BorderRank_x_PhaseTransition'. Underscores and CamelCase mixed acceptable when describing tier × tier compositions.

precondition_primitives.title: Primitives that must exist for this composition to apply
precondition_primitives.description: Two or more substrate primitives whose composition produces output_primitive. Each must be UpperCamelCase; each must already exist in the substrate or be a primitive_proposal in the same batch.

output_primitive.description: The substrate primitive produced by composing the preconditions. May or may not be a primitive_proposal in the same batch — composition rules can predate the existence of their output primitive.

literature_confirmation.title: Literature confirmations of this composition
literature_confirmation.description: Each confirmation is an object {citation, context}. Substrate-grade confirmation requires 2+ INDEPENDENT confirmations (substrate-tester saturation rule). The 'confirmed' boolean below indicates whether that threshold is met.

context.description: Free-form context: where in the paper this composition appears, which fire surfaced it, T#NN entry it was associated with.

confirmed.title: Has the saturation rule been met (≥2 independent confirmations)?
confirmed.description: true ONLY if literature_confirmation has 2+ entries from independent sources (different author groups, different papers). The validator does NOT enforce the 2+ count automatically because 'independent' is a judgment call; the reviewer asserts.

examples.name: TierB_x_TierD_BorderRank_x_PhaseTransition

literature_confirmation.context: T#73 fire #43 — Hopkins-Steurer SOS lower bound for tensor PCA at fixed (n,d,lambda) is the Tier-B SOS certificate; the threshold curve is the Tier-D

examples.notes: First independent confirmation of Tier-B + Tier-D cross-tier composition. Tensor-PCA threshold cluster: BorderRankWitness composes with PhaseTransitionThreshold to certify a constructive existence claim that holds within a stated distributional regime.
